texto: Stepping back and connecting reconciliation to God’s story also helps us move away from dramatic visions of fixing the world, as if our job were to provide solutions to problems outside us. If Christians believe anything, it is that no one—including ourselves and the church—is separate from the brokenness as an untainted solution to the problems of our world. The new creation contends with the old. The dividing line between good and evil runs straight through each one of us. So the journey of reconciliation begins with a transformation of the human person.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Chris Rice: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a trabalho. O trecho “Stepping back and connecting reconciliation to God’s story also helps us move away from dramatic …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que trabalho, jesus, reconciliation pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e viagem.
